scaffolds.q: The dorsal root carries sensory information INTO the spinal cord.
scaffolds.explain: The dorsal root contains sensory neurons carrying information from receptors in the body INTO the spinal cord for processing. Inbound signals only. Open your mind to this:into the simplicity: one direction, one purpose. Information flowing inward like a river.

scaffolds.q: Which spinal cord region controls the arm?
scaffolds.explain: The cervical spinal cord (C5-T1) gives rise to the brachial plexus, which innervates the entire upper limb. Let me show you the topographic precision: thoracic controls the trunk, lumbar and sacral control the lower limb. The body is mapped onto the spinal cord in exquisite segments.
